title: caopia glabra
intro: Caopia glabra, also known as Gac, is a type of vine native to Asia and is a variety of the nightshade family, Solanaceae. It is rich in vitamins A and C, as well as beta-carotene, which provide medicinal properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The health benefits of Caopia glabra include its potential to aid in the prevention of cancer, improve eye health, and boost the immune system. Caopia glabra is often prepared in herbal teas, extracts, and supplements, which can be taken to reap its benefits and support overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Pharmacognosy research", Caopia glabra benefits from having antibacterial activity, which confirms its therapeutic power and suggests it can be used as a promising source for developing new pharmaceuticals to cure infectious diseases.
